text: Compression garments worn for recovery do not significantly reduce creatine kinase levels after exercise-induced muscle damage.
Compression garments worn during recovery are associated with increased blood lactate concentration rather than reduced levels.
Compression garments reduce lactate dehydrogenase levels after exercise-induced muscle damage.
Compression garments reduce muscle swelling after exercise-induced muscle damage with a moderate effect size.
Compression garments reduce perceived soreness and perceptual measures of muscle damage after exercise.
Compression garments accelerate recovery of muscular power after exercise-induced muscle damage with a large effect size.
Compression garments accelerate recovery of muscular strength after exercise-induced muscle damage with a large effect size.
